scenarioName: TED Tender Alerts from a Persistent Apify Task

modules.capInvariant: This specification is intentionally non-paginated. The saved Task maxNewPerRun must be no greater than 999; the Actor appends exactly one summary control row, so 999 tender rows plus 1 summary fit the fixed retrieval limit of 1000.

configuration.targetStructure: all TED record fields required by modules 7 and 8

record.title: display only: title when non-empty, otherwise Tender <publicationNumber>
record.payloadJson: JSON string of the original dataset record; title:null remains null here

modules.nativeIdempotency: Add/Replace with overwrite enabled leaves exactly one record for the stable key, including after an ambiguous retry.

record.sourceId: run ID plus Iterator bundle order
record.title: display only: Tender <publicationNumber> or TED <recordType> record
record.payloadJson: JSON string of the original dataset record; title:null remains null here

modules.nativeIdempotency: Add/Replace with overwrite enabled leaves exactly one record for the deterministic run-row key, including after an ambiguous retry.

modules.purpose: Record that the malformed tender was persisted under its deterministic run-row fallback key but is not exposed as a tender.
Report the non-success terminal status only after module 9 persisted the run-scoped TED dataset row.
Report the non-success terminal status only after the identified TED tender has been idempotently persisted.
Report a non-success terminal run whose fetched dataset contains no rows; no destination write is attempted.
Report any terminal run without a defaultDatasetId and end safely without requesting dataset items.

terminalFailureRoute.identifiedTenderRows: module 13 runs after module 8 persisted ted:<publicationNumber>
terminalFailureRoute.runScopedRows: module 12 runs from module 10 after module 9 persisted ted:run:<runId>:row:<bundleOrder>
terminalFailureRoute.emptyDataset: module 14 reports the fetched zero-row dataset
terminalFailureRoute.missingDataset: module 15 does not request dataset items
terminalFailureRoute.then: end each instant-triggered branch successfully after exposing the terminal failure diagnostic; do not throw an error that could disable the scenario

invalidTenderRoute.source: module 10 invalidTenderDiagnostic after module 9 run-scoped persistence
invalidTenderRoute.then: record the missing publicationNumber and fallback key; do not expose the malformed row as a tender

nonTenderRoute: Prime and summary records are persisted under deterministic ted:run:<runId>:row:<bundleOrder> keys but are not exposed as tenders.

errorHandlers.scenario: Set Store incomplete executions to Yes.

getDatasetItems.temporaryErrors: ConnectionError, RateLimitError, and ModuleTimeoutError use Make's automatic exponential-backoff schedule; other errors captured by Retry use the configured 3 attempts at 15-minute intervals.

destination.resumeAt: the failed Data store module with the same dataset bundle
destination.requiredProof: Inject a timeout after each Data store commit, retry module 8 with ted:<publicationNumber> and module 9 with ted:run:<runId>:row:<bundleOrder>, and verify exactly one record exists for each stable product-prefixed key.

publicationGate: Build in a user-owned Make account, confirm saved Task maxNewPerRun is no greater than 999 so 999 tender rows plus the Actor's one summary control row fit the fixed non-paginated retrieval limit of 1000, and set Store incomplete executions to Yes.

Run tender, quiet, prime/summary, invalid-tender, FAILED, ABORTED, TIMED-OUT, missing-dataset-ID, dataset-retry, and Data-store-retry tests; prove module 8 writes only ted:<publicationNumber> and module 9 writes only ted:run:<runId>:row:<bundleOrder>, both with overwrite enabled.
Inject a post-commit timeout independently at modules 8 and 9 and prove retry leaves exactly one record for each route's stable key without rerunning the Apify Task.
Export and scrub the candidate blueprint.
Import that exact file into a fresh empty validation organization/team or clean account.
Reconnect credentials, save and activate the scenario, and verify Make recreated the Task-scoped Watch Task Runs webhook.

Repeat tender, quiet, prime/summary, invalid-tender, FAILED, ABORTED, TIMED-OUT, missing-dataset-ID, dataset-retry, and both module 8 and module 9 Data-store-retry tests after exact-file import, including exactly one record for each key after its post-commit timeout.
Obtain independent validator PASS and separate adversarial PASS before publication.
